steps.canonical_text: Check FAST right now: ask them to smile for face droop, raise both arms for arm weakness, and repeat a short sentence for slurred or wrong words.
Note the exact time symptoms started, or the last time they were seen normal, and keep that time ready for emergency services.
must_keep_keywords: last time they were seen normal

steps.canonical_text: Call emergency services now even if symptoms seem to improve. Say you suspect a stroke and give the symptom start time. Every minute matters in stroke.
Help them rest and keep them still. If alert, sit them up with the head and shoulders supported. If drowsy or vomiting, lay them on their side.
Do not give food, drink, or any medication, including aspirin. Swallowing may be impaired.
Stay with them and keep checking breathing and alertness. If they become unresponsive and stop breathing normally, start chest compressions.
